key_insight: The paper reformulates hyperelastic and active tissue mechanics as an edge-strain interaction potential, bridging continuum fidelity and fast discrete solvers.
engineering_relevance: This enables efficient simulation of active biological tissues, such as the myocardium, without relying on expensive global nonlinear finite element solves at every time step.
method: Recast hyperelastic constitutive laws using tetrahedral edge strain.
Embed active-strain contraction via a time-dependent activated reference configuration.
Add an incompressibility strategy that avoids volumetric locking while preserving energetic coupling between edges.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical tests across multiple hyperelastic laws, energy-balance preservation, and a realistic ventricle case reproducing literature-consistent longitudinal shortening and wall thickening.
watch_item: As an edge-based continuum representation, accuracy and robustness will depend on mesh quality, incompressibility enforcement, and careful calibration of the active-strain model.
paper_title: An interaction potential method for passive and active dynamics of hyperelastic materials
